sections.title: Market Pulse: Bullish Momentum Continues Amid Strong Earnings

items.headline: Wall Street Extends Winning Streak as Earnings Outperform
items.summary: U.S. equities closed out an eighth consecutive winning week on Friday, driven by a robust corporate earnings season. The S&P 500, NASDAQ, and Dow Jones Industrial Average all posted gains, with the Dow reaching a new record high. Despite the bullish market, consumer sentiment dipped to a record low due to persistent inflation concerns.

items.statContext: Up 0.58% Friday, 2.1% for the week (Record High)

items.headline: Goldman Sachs Group Soars on SpaceX News
items.summary: Goldman Sachs Group ($GS) advanced 5.09% for the week to $996.73, benefiting from reports that SpaceX selected the bank to lead a potentially record-breaking offering.

items.headline: IREN Jumps on Nvidia H100 GPU Rental Prices
items.summary: IREN ($IREN) surged 7.35% for the week to $56.83. Shares gained traction after Nvidia revealed continued increases in rental prices for its H100 GPUs.

items.headline: Workday Beats Q1 Earnings Estimates
items.summary: Workday ($WDAY) rose 5.2% on Friday after the company reported stronger first-quarter profits than analysts had expected.

items.headline: Futu Holdings Declines Amid Regulatory Scrutiny
items.summary: Futu Holdings ($FUTU) led mid-cap losers, falling 33.33% for the week to $89.76. The decline follows a Chinese securities regulator's investigation into unlicensed cross-border trading.

items.headline: Pershing Square Retreats
items.summary: Pershing Square ($PS) was a significant loser, retreating 13.36% for the week to $35.67.

items.headline: American Express Affected by Consumer Sentiment Drop
items.summary: American Express ($AXP) retreated 0.54% for the week to $311.78, impacted by a reported decline in consumer sentiment.

items.headline: S&P 500 Firms Universally Beat Earnings Expectations
items.summary: All reporting S&P 500 companies in Q1 2026 exceeded earnings expectations, with 79% also achieving year-over-year growth. Notable beats included Home Depot, Walmart, Lowe's, Nvidia, Deere & Company, and Target.

items.headline: NVIDIA's Strong Earnings Report Fuels AI Momentum
items.summary: NVIDIA Corporation ($NVDA) surpassed fiscal first-quarter 2027 consensus estimates, driven by a substantial increase in hyperscaler AI infrastructure capital expenditures. The company's forward guidance signals continued market dominance in the semiconductor industry.

items.headline: Consumer Sentiment Hits Record Low Amid Inflation Worries
items.summary: U.S. consumer sentiment declined for the third consecutive month in May, reaching a record low. This downturn is largely attributed to anxieties over inflation, exacerbated by the conflict in Iran and rising gasoline prices. Year-ahead inflation expectations increased to 4.8%.

items.headline: Healthcare and Energy Lead as Tech Rebounds
items.summary: Healthcare experienced its best week in over six months, gaining more than 3%. Energy remained strong, while the technology sector continued its rebound, emerging as the second-best performing sector year-to-date. Investors are also rotating into REITs, Consumer Cyclical, and value equities.

items.headline: U.S. Mortgage Rates Climb to Nine-Month High
items.summary: The average long-term U.S. mortgage rate rose to its highest level in nearly nine months, with the 30-year fixed rate reaching 6.51%. This increase is impacting homebuying costs during a traditionally busy period for the housing market.
